reasoning: All 7 `remove_container_preserve_tree_inv_*` lemmas are `external_body`, meaning the entire proof that container removal preserves tree invariants is axiomatically trusted. This is a real soundness gap — any bug in the `remove_container_ensures` spec would go undetected.

`remove_container_ensures` never requires `owned_threads@ =~= Set::empty()` as a precondition. The container is removed from the domain while potentially still owning threads, which are silently orphaned with no transfer or cleanup requirement.

Same pattern — `remove_container_ensures` never requires `owned_endpoints@ =~= Set::empty()`. Endpoints are silently orphaned when the container is removed, violating the code comment's intent that endpoints should only be referenced within the subtree.
`remove_container_ensures` never requires `owned_procs@ =~= Seq::empty()`. Processes are silently orphaned when the container is removed from the domain.

The spec explicitly handles this correctly. `remove_container_ensures` includes: for all `c_ptr` in `uppertree_seq`, `new_container_perms[c_ptr].subtree_set@ =~= old_container_perms[c_ptr].subtree_set@.remove(container_ptr)`. The ensures merely restates what the spec already guarantees — this is a desirable property, not a gap.
